ogTitle: Mountain River vs Murdunna: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mountain River and Murdunna.
heading: Mountain River vs Murdunna — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mountain River, Tasmania and Murdunna, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Murdunna has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Mountain River (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mountain River or Murdunna safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mountain River has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Murdunna scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mountain River scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Murdunna scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mountain River or Murdunna?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Murdunna has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
